speaker:
KING
               
              PHILIP
text: So, by a roaring tempest on the flood,

speaker:
KING
               
              PHILIP
text: A whole armada of convicted sail

speaker:
KING
               
              PHILIP
text: Is scattered and disjoined from fellowship.

speaker: PANDULPH
text: Courage and comfort. All shall yet go well.

speaker:
KING
               
              PHILIP
text: What can go well when we have run so ill?

speaker:
KING
               
              PHILIP
text: Are we not beaten? Is not Angiers lost?

speaker:
KING
               
              PHILIP
text: Arthur ta’en prisoner? Divers dear friends slain?

speaker:
KING
               
              PHILIP
text: And bloody England into England gone,

speaker:
KING
               
              PHILIP
text: O’erbearing interruption, spite of France?

speaker: DAUPHIN
text: What he hath won, that hath he fortified.

speaker: DAUPHIN
text: So hot a speed, with such advice disposed,

speaker: DAUPHIN
text: Such temperate order in so fierce a cause,

speaker: DAUPHIN
text: Doth want example. Who hath read or heard

speaker: DAUPHIN
text: Of any kindred action like to this?

speaker:
KING
               
              PHILIP
text: Well could I bear that England had this praise,

speaker:
KING
               
              PHILIP
text: So we could find some pattern of our shame.

speaker:
KING
               
              PHILIP
text: Look who comes here! A grave unto a soul,

speaker:
KING
               
              PHILIP
text: Holding th’ eternal spirit against her will

speaker:
KING
               
              PHILIP
text: In the vile prison of afflicted breath.—

speaker:
KING
               
              PHILIP
text: I prithee, lady, go away with me.

speaker: CONSTANCE
text: Lo, now, now see the issue of your peace!

speaker:
KING
               
              PHILIP
text: Patience, good lady. Comfort, gentle Constance.

speaker: CONSTANCE
text: No, I defy all counsel, all redress,

speaker: CONSTANCE
text: But that which ends all counsel, true redress.

speaker: CONSTANCE
text: Death, death, O amiable, lovely death,

speaker: CONSTANCE
text: Thou odoriferous stench, sound rottenness,

speaker: CONSTANCE
text: Arise forth from the couch of lasting night,

speaker: CONSTANCE
text: Thou hate and terror to prosperity,

speaker: CONSTANCE
text: And I will kiss thy detestable bones

speaker: CONSTANCE
text: And put my eyeballs in thy vaulty brows,

speaker: CONSTANCE
text: And ring these fingers with thy household worms,

speaker: CONSTANCE
text: And stop this gap of breath with fulsome dust,

speaker: CONSTANCE
text: And be a carrion monster like thyself.

speaker: CONSTANCE
text: Come, grin on me, and I will think thou smil’st,

speaker: CONSTANCE
text: And buss thee as thy wife. Misery’s love,

speaker: CONSTANCE
text: O, come to me!

speaker:
KING
               
              PHILIP
text: O fair affliction, peace!

speaker: CONSTANCE
text: No, no, I will not, having breath to cry.

speaker: CONSTANCE
text: O, that my tongue were in the thunder’s mouth!

speaker: CONSTANCE
text: Then with a passion would I shake the world

speaker: CONSTANCE
text: And rouse from sleep that fell anatomy

speaker: CONSTANCE
text: Which cannot hear a lady’s feeble voice,

speaker: CONSTANCE
text: Which scorns a modern invocation.

speaker: PANDULPH
text: Lady, you utter madness and not sorrow.

speaker: CONSTANCE
text: Thou art not holy to belie me so.

speaker: CONSTANCE
text: I am not mad. This hair I tear is mine;

speaker: CONSTANCE
text: My name is Constance; I was Geoffrey’s wife;

speaker: CONSTANCE
text: Young Arthur is my son, and he is lost.

speaker: CONSTANCE
text: I am not mad; I would to heaven I were,

speaker: CONSTANCE
text: For then ’tis like I should forget myself.

speaker: CONSTANCE
text: O, if I could, what grief should I forget!

speaker: CONSTANCE
text: Preach some philosophy to make me mad,

speaker: CONSTANCE
text: And thou shalt be canonized, cardinal.

speaker: CONSTANCE
text: For, being not mad but sensible of grief,

speaker: CONSTANCE
text: My reasonable part produces reason

speaker: CONSTANCE
text: How I may be delivered of these woes,

speaker: CONSTANCE
text: And teaches me to kill or hang myself.

speaker: CONSTANCE
text: If I were mad, I should forget my son,

speaker: CONSTANCE
text: Or madly think a babe of clouts were he.

speaker: CONSTANCE
text: I am not mad. Too well, too well I feel

speaker: CONSTANCE
text: The different plague of each calamity.

speaker:
KING
               
              PHILIP
text: Bind up those tresses.—O, what love I note

speaker:
KING
               
              PHILIP
text: In the fair multitude of those her hairs;

speaker:
KING
               
              PHILIP
text: Where but by chance a silver drop hath fall’n,

speaker:
KING
               
              PHILIP
text: Even to that drop ten thousand wiry friends

speaker:
KING
               
              PHILIP
text: Do glue themselves in sociable grief,

speaker:
KING
               
              PHILIP
text: Like true, inseparable, faithful loves,

speaker:
KING
               
              PHILIP
text: Sticking together in calamity.

speaker: CONSTANCE
text: To England, if you will.

speaker:
KING
               
              PHILIP
text: Bind up your hairs.

speaker: CONSTANCE
text: Yes, that I will. And wherefore will I do it?

speaker: CONSTANCE
text: I tore them from their bonds and cried aloud

speaker: CONSTANCE
text: “O, that these hands could so redeem my son,

speaker: CONSTANCE
text: As they have given these hairs their liberty!”

speaker: CONSTANCE
text: But now I envy at their liberty,

speaker: CONSTANCE
text: And will again commit them to their bonds,

speaker: CONSTANCE
text: Because my poor child is a prisoner.

speaker: CONSTANCE
text: And father cardinal, I have heard you say

speaker: CONSTANCE
text: That we shall see and know our friends in heaven.

speaker: CONSTANCE
text: If that be true, I shall see my boy again;

speaker: CONSTANCE
text: For since the birth of Cain, the first male child,

speaker: CONSTANCE
text: To him that did but yesterday suspire,

speaker: CONSTANCE
text: There was not such a gracious creature born.

speaker: CONSTANCE
text: But now will canker sorrow eat my bud

speaker: CONSTANCE
text: And chase the native beauty from his cheek,

speaker: CONSTANCE
text: And he will look as hollow as a ghost,

speaker: CONSTANCE
text: As dim and meager as an ague’s fit,

speaker: CONSTANCE
text: And so he’ll die; and, rising so again,

speaker: CONSTANCE
text: When I shall meet him in the court of heaven

speaker: CONSTANCE
text: I shall not know him. Therefore never, never

speaker: CONSTANCE
text: Must I behold my pretty Arthur more.

speaker: PANDULPH
text: You hold too heinous a respect of grief.

speaker: CONSTANCE
text: He talks to me that never had a son.

speaker:
KING
               
              PHILIP
text: You are as fond of grief as of your child.

speaker: CONSTANCE
text: Grief fills the room up of my absent child,

speaker: CONSTANCE
text: Lies in his bed, walks up and down with me,

speaker: CONSTANCE
text: Puts on his pretty looks, repeats his words,

speaker: CONSTANCE
text: Remembers me of all his gracious parts,

speaker: CONSTANCE
text: Stuffs out his vacant garments with his form;

speaker: CONSTANCE
text: Then, have I reason to be fond of grief?

speaker: CONSTANCE
text: Fare you well. Had you such a loss as I,

speaker: CONSTANCE
text: I could give better comfort than you do.

speaker: CONSTANCE
text: I will not keep this form upon my head

speaker: CONSTANCE
text: When there is such disorder in my wit.

speaker: CONSTANCE
text: O Lord! My boy, my Arthur, my fair son,

speaker: CONSTANCE
text: My life, my joy, my food, my all the world,

speaker: CONSTANCE
text: My widow-comfort and my sorrows’ cure!

speaker:
KING
               
              PHILIP
text: I fear some outrage, and I’ll follow her.

speaker: DAUPHIN
text: There’s nothing in this world can make me joy.

speaker: DAUPHIN
text: Life is as tedious as a twice-told tale,

speaker: DAUPHIN
text: Vexing the dull ear of a drowsy man;

speaker: DAUPHIN
text: And bitter shame hath spoiled the sweet world’s taste,

speaker: DAUPHIN
text: That it yields naught but shame and bitterness.

speaker: PANDULPH
text: Before the curing of a strong disease,

speaker: PANDULPH
text: Even in the instant of repair and health,

speaker: PANDULPH
text: The fit is strongest. Evils that take leave

speaker: PANDULPH
text: On their departure most of all show evil.

speaker: PANDULPH
text: What have you lost by losing of this day?

speaker: DAUPHIN
text: All days of glory, joy, and happiness.

speaker: PANDULPH
text: If you had won it, certainly you had.

speaker: PANDULPH
text: No, no. When Fortune means to men most good,

speaker: PANDULPH
text: She looks upon them with a threat’ning eye.

speaker: PANDULPH
text: ’Tis strange to think how much King John hath lost

speaker: PANDULPH
text: In this which he accounts so clearly won.

speaker: PANDULPH
text: Are not you grieved that Arthur is his prisoner?

speaker: DAUPHIN
text: As heartily as he is glad he hath him.

speaker: PANDULPH
text: Your mind is all as youthful as your blood.

speaker: PANDULPH
text: Now hear me speak with a prophetic spirit.

speaker: PANDULPH
text: For even the breath of what I mean to speak

speaker: PANDULPH
text: Shall blow each dust, each straw, each little rub,

speaker: PANDULPH
text: Out of the path which shall directly lead

speaker: PANDULPH
text: Thy foot to England’s throne. And therefore mark:

speaker: PANDULPH
text: John hath seized Arthur, and it cannot be

speaker: PANDULPH
text: That, whiles warm life plays in that infant’s veins,

speaker: PANDULPH
text: The misplaced John should entertain an hour,

speaker: PANDULPH
text: One minute, nay, one quiet breath of rest.

speaker: PANDULPH
text: A scepter snatched with an unruly hand

speaker: PANDULPH
text: Must be as boisterously maintained as gained.

speaker: PANDULPH
text: And he that stands upon a slipp’ry place

speaker: PANDULPH
text: Makes nice of no vile hold to stay him up.

speaker: PANDULPH
text: That John may stand, then Arthur needs must fall.

speaker: PANDULPH
text: So be it, for it cannot be but so.

speaker: DAUPHIN
text: But what shall I gain by young Arthur’s fall?

speaker: PANDULPH
text: You, in the right of Lady Blanche your wife,

speaker: PANDULPH
text: May then make all the claim that Arthur did.

speaker: DAUPHIN
text: And lose it, life and all, as Arthur did.

speaker: PANDULPH
text: How green you are and fresh in this old world!

speaker: PANDULPH
text: John lays you plots. The times conspire with you,

speaker: PANDULPH
text: For he that steeps his safety in true blood

speaker: PANDULPH
text: Shall find but bloody safety, and untrue.

speaker: PANDULPH
text: This act so evilly borne shall cool the hearts

speaker: PANDULPH
text: Of all his people and freeze up their zeal,

speaker: PANDULPH
text: That none so small advantage shall step forth

speaker: PANDULPH
text: To check his reign but they will cherish it.

speaker: PANDULPH
text: No natural exhalation in the sky,

speaker: PANDULPH
text: No scope of nature, no distempered day,

speaker: PANDULPH
text: No common wind, no customèd event,

speaker: PANDULPH
text: But they will pluck away his natural cause

speaker: PANDULPH
text: And call them meteors, prodigies, and signs,

speaker: PANDULPH
text: Abortives, presages, and tongues of heaven,

speaker: PANDULPH
text: Plainly denouncing vengeance upon John.

speaker: DAUPHIN
text: Maybe he will not touch young Arthur’s life,

speaker: DAUPHIN
text: But hold himself safe in his prisonment.

speaker: PANDULPH
text: O, sir, when he shall hear of your approach,

speaker: PANDULPH
text: If that young Arthur be not gone already,

speaker: PANDULPH
text: Even at that news he dies; and then the hearts

speaker: PANDULPH
text: Of all his people shall revolt from him

speaker: PANDULPH
text: And kiss the lips of unacquainted change,

speaker: PANDULPH
text: And pick strong matter of revolt and wrath

speaker: PANDULPH
text: Out of the bloody fingers’ ends of John.

speaker: PANDULPH
text: Methinks I see this hurly all on foot;

speaker: PANDULPH
text: And, O, what better matter breeds for you

speaker: PANDULPH
text: Than I have named! The bastard Faulconbridge

speaker: PANDULPH
text: Is now in England ransacking the Church,

speaker: PANDULPH
text: Offending charity. If but a dozen French

speaker: PANDULPH
text: Were there in arms, they would be as a call

speaker: PANDULPH
text: To train ten thousand English to their side,

speaker: PANDULPH
text: Or as a little snow, tumbled about,

speaker: PANDULPH
text: Anon becomes a mountain. O noble dauphin,

speaker: PANDULPH
text: Go with me to the King. ’Tis wonderful

speaker: PANDULPH
text: What may be wrought out of their discontent,

speaker: PANDULPH
text: Now that their souls are topful of offense.

speaker: PANDULPH
text: For England, go. I will whet on the King.

speaker: DAUPHIN
text: Strong reasons makes strange actions. Let us go.

speaker: DAUPHIN
text: If you say ay, the King will not say no.
